attributes.description: Alerts when in-flight messages approach the standard-queue limit (~120,000), indicating stuck consumers or processing bottlenecks.
attributes.name: [AWS SQS OTel] In-flight saturation

investigation_guide.blob:
## In-Flight Saturation

`ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible` counts messages received but not yet deleted (hidden by visibility timeout). Standard queues cap in-flight messages at ~120,000; approaching this limit blocks new deliveries.

### Triage Steps
1. Check whether consumers are deleting messages — compare `NumberOfMessagesDeleted` to `NumberOfMessagesReceived`.
2. Look for stuck or crashed consumers holding messages until visibility timeout expires.
3. Verify visibility timeout is not excessively long relative to processing time.
4. Inspect consumer error rates — are messages failing without being deleted or sent to a DLQ?
5. Consider scaling consumers or reducing batch receive sizes if processing is slow.

### Customisation
- Tune the in-flight threshold relative to the standard-queue limit (~120,000); the default alerts at roughly 83% of that limit.
- FIFO queues have different in-flight limits — adjust threshold per queue type if needed.
- This rule uses the `Average` stat, per AWS recommended alarms for `ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible`.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-aws.sqs.otel-*
| WHERE Namespace == "AWS/SQS"
    AND MetricName == "ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible"
    AND stat == "Average"
    AND QueueName NOT LIKE "*dlq*"
| STATS in_flight = AVG(`metrics.amazonaws.com/AWS/SQS/ApproximateNumberOfMessagesNotVisible`)
    BY QueueName, cloud.region
// Standard queues cap at ~120k in-flight; tune threshold (100000 = ~83% of limit)
| WHERE in_flight > 100000
